analytics.name: analytics

management_commands.name: compute_outcomes
management_commands.help: Recompute PatientOutcome rows (the outcome engine).

analytics.models_module_doc:
PatientOutcome — the computed "Outcome Engine" row, one per patient.

Per the registry's guiding principle, outcomes are NOT entered by hand: this row
is derived from the longitudinal labs, the treatment exposures, and the dated
clinical events, with the operational endpoint definitions fixed in code
(analytics/services/outcomes.py). Re-runnable any time via `compute_outcomes`.

It is also the denormalized, ML-ready "one row per patient" table the portfolio
asks for, and the source of the (duration, event) pairs the survival analysis
consumes.

management_commands.name: seed_roles
management_commands.help: Create roles (Groups) and assign per-model permissions.

audit.name: audit

management_commands.name: restore_backup
management_commands.help: Restore the database from a backup ZIP (or legacy .sqlite3 snapshot).

audit.models_module_doc:
AuditLog  — append-only per-field change history (who / when / old -> new).
Consent   — versioned, withdrawable patient consent.

These are the two pieces a prospective registry needs before it can feed
registry-embedded trials: a defensible record of every data change, and a
consent trail with versions and withdrawal.

baseline.name: baseline
baseline.models_module_doc:
BaselineAssessment — the at-enrollment clinical/demographic snapshot (Excel
sheet 2). One per patient. BMI and its Asian-cutoff category are auto-derived.

biobank.name: biobank
biobank.models_module_doc:
Biobank — serial sample storage (serum, plasma, urine, DNA, tissue) for the
future biomarker work (Gd-IgA1, complement, genetics) the portfolio describes.

Every sample requires biobank consent. The gate is enforced in clean() so admin
forms validate, and in store_sample() for programmatic use.

biomarkers.name: biomarkers

management_commands.name: compute_biomarkers
management_commands.help: Recompute BiomarkerKinetics rows from the lab series.

biomarkers.models_module_doc:
Biomarker kinetics (protocol §9.3, portfolio Study 6).

Computed per-patient from the longitudinal LabResult series — like PatientOutcome,
not hand-entered. Headline is anti-PLA2R in membranous nephropathy:

  * >=50% decline (early decline = strong predictor of later remission)
  * seroconversion to negative = IMMUNOLOGICAL REMISSION (precedes proteinuria
    remission), with first-achieved dates so it is a time-to-event endpoint

Plus complement recovery (C3/C4 normalisation in LN/C3G) and anti-dsDNA
normalisation (LN).

clinical_reasoning.name: clinical_reasoning

management_commands.name: deactivate_test_rules
management_commands.help: Deactivate all TEST-* knowledge base rules to draft status

clinical_reasoning.models_module_doc:
Clinical Reasoning Engine models.

Continuously updated per-patient profiles aggregating data from all
clinical modules into structured reasoning artifacts.

encounters.name: encounters
encounters.models_module_doc:
ClinicalEncounter — the hub of the real-world workflow. One encounter per visit.
The prescription, the follow-up clinical data, and the lab orders all hang off
it, so the single act of "seeing the patient" populates the whole registry.

FollowUpVisit here is intentionally light: the longitudinal clinical fields the
research portfolio needs. In the full build it would carry the complete sheet-6
variable set; the prescription module only needs the encounter spine.

events.name: events
events.models_module_doc: Event orchestration models — persisted event log and subscriptions.

management_commands.name: export_feedback_package
management_commands.help: Export a de-identified feedback package ZIP

management_commands.name: import_feedback
management_commands.help: Import feedback packages for analysis (developer PC)

management_commands.name: upload_logs
management_commands.help: Upload pending error reports to GitHub

management_commands.name: followup_engine
management_commands.help: Interact with the follow-up engine

management_commands.help: Activate DRAFT knowledge base entries

management_commands.name: backfill_governance
management_commands.help: Backfill missing governance metadata on KB entries

management_commands.name: export_knowledge_base
management_commands.help: Export knowledge base entries to a JSON file

management_commands.name: import_guideline
management_commands.help: Import rules from a guideline file (JSON, YAML, CSV, markdown)

management_commands.name: knowledge_dashboard
management_commands.help: Display the Knowledge Quality Dashboard

management_commands.name: load_test_knowledge
management_commands.help: Load deterministic test knowledge base for integration testing

management_commands.name: run_rule_tests
management_commands.help: Run tests against active knowledge base rules

management_commands.name: seed_clinical_cases
management_commands.help: Seed V4.0 clinical case library for validation

management_commands.name: seed_drug_intelligence
management_commands.help: Seed/refresh the DrugIntelligence knowledge base for GN drugs (idempotent).

management_commands.name: seed_knowledge_base
management_commands.help: Seed the knowledge base with expanded disease profiles (200+ rules)

management_commands.name: seed_missing_diseases
management_commands.help: Scaffold missing diseases + DRAFT rules (for expert authoring).

management_commands.name: seed_v4_knowledge
management_commands.help: Seed V4.0 medical knowledge expansion: diseases, guidelines, pathways, drugs

management_commands.name: validate_governance
management_commands.help: Validate governance metadata on ACTIVE knowledge base entries

management_commands.name: validate_rules
management_commands.help: Validate knowledge base entries for data integrity

labs.name: labs

management_commands.name: seed_labs
management_commands.help: Seed lab test catalog and ordering panels.

labs.models_module_doc:
Longitudinal laboratory model.

Real-world flow: a lab is ORDERED at one visit and RESULTED at a later date.
So results cannot live as flat baseline columns — they are time-series rows, one
per (patient, test, date). This is what feeds the trajectories the research
portfolio depends on: eGFR slope, anti-PLA2R kinetics, proteinuria trends.

    LabTest      — the test catalog (LOINC-ready), reference ranges, units
    LabPanel     — a named bundle of tests for one-click ordering
    LabOrder     — placed at a ClinicalEncounter (the "record the lab" step)
    LabOrderItem — one ordered test; gets fulfilled by a LabResult
    LabResult    — the longitudinal value; eGFR is auto-derived from creatinine

pathology.name: pathology
pathology.models_module_doc:
Histopathology (Excel sheet 4) — the research-grade biopsy dataset.

    Biopsy              core biopsy + lesion descriptors
    GNDiagnosis         the diagnosis (specific + broad + pathogenesis groups)
    IgANScore           Oxford MEST-C (discrete fields, per the portfolio)
    LupusPathology      ISN/RPS class + NIH activity/chronicity indices
    FSGSPathology       primary/secondary + histologic variant
    MembranousPathology PLA2R/THSD7A tissue staining + MN stage
    BiopsyImage         digital-pathology slide images (imaging-consent gated)

Capturing the scores as discrete ordinal fields (not buried in narrative) is the
single change that makes the biopsy archive research-grade.

patients.name: patients

management_commands.name: backup_db
management_commands.help: Create a timestamped backup of the database (or list existing backups).

management_commands.name: check_data_integrity
management_commands.help: Run comprehensive data integrity checks across all clinical models.

management_commands.name: delete_patient
management_commands.help: Delete a patient and all their clinical data.

management_commands.name: reset_demo_data
management_commands.help: Remove demo/synthetic patient data (preview unless --yes).

management_commands.name: restore_db
management_commands.help: Restore the database from a backup file (overwrites the current DB).

management_commands.name: validate_patients
management_commands.help: Validate patient registry: dedupe, missing fields, coverage gaps.

patients.models_module_doc:
Minimal Patient model — just enough of the core registry for the prescription
module to hang off. The full registry (baseline, labs, pathology, outcomes)
plugs in around this without changing the prescription workflow.

prescriptions.name: prescriptions

management_commands.name: seed_drugs
management_commands.help: Seed the nephrology DrugMaster formulary (Bangladeshi brands).

prescriptions.models_module_doc:
Prescription   — the printable clinical artifact, one per encounter, versioned
                 and immutable once finalized (medico-legal + audit).
PrescriptionItem — a single "what to take now" line. Because we print the FULL
                 current medication list each visit, the set of items at finalize
                 time IS the patient's standing regimen — which is exactly what
                 the reconciliation engine diffs against open exposures.

safety.name: safety
safety.models_module_doc:
Adverse-event / safety capture (protocol §9.4).

The registry's safety co-primary: serious adverse events, infections (a major
burden in Bangladesh and under immunosuppression), steroid toxicity, and the
counts the DSMB reviews. Coded so the portfolio's infection studies (Study 20:
infection risk by agent and diabetes status; Study 23: diabetic IRGN) are
answerable, and so per-study SAE rates can be tabulated by arm.

This is distinct from encounters.ClinicalEvent (efficacy endpoints like ESKD /
death / remission) — AdverseEvent is the harms side.

scheduling.name: scheduling
scheduling.models_module_doc:
Follow-up scheduling (protocol §7.6).

A prospective GN registry runs on a defined visit cadence delivered at the weekly
Tuesday GN clinic (max 15 participants/session), with each visit scheduled within
a ±7-day window of its protocol timepoint, and early safety visits (weeks 1, 2, 4)
for participants on active immunosuppression.

ScheduledVisit is one protocol-mandated slot per patient per timepoint. An
encounter (the actual visit) fulfils it. This is the operational layer that lets
the coordinator see who is due, who is overdue, and the roster for a given clinic
day — replacing the spreadsheet's Patient_Tracking / Followup_Schedule sheets.

studies.name: studies

management_commands.name: auto_screen_patients
management_commands.help: Run automatic study eligibility screening across all patients.

management_commands.name: seed_studies
management_commands.help: Seed the BGDDR research portfolio as selectable studies.

studies.models_module_doc:
Registry-embedded trial platform.

    Study           — a study/trial definition (observational, quasi-exp, or RCT)
    StudyArm        — its arms, each with an allocation weight
    StudyEnrollment — a patient's screening + (for RCTs) randomized allocation

Designed so the registry doubles as a trial platform: enrol eligible patients,
randomize them with a reproducible stratified-block scheme, and then analyse the
arms with the existing survival / Cox engine (group_by="study:<code>").

treatments.name: treatments

management_commands.name: seed_drug_knowledge
management_commands.help: Seed V4.0 drug knowledge: mechanism, side effects, monitoring, dosage

treatments.models_module_doc:
DrugMaster      — the clinic formulary AND the research-coding bridge.
TreatmentExposure — one row per *episode* of a drug at a dose. This is the
research-grade table the portfolio depends on (new-user cohorts, target-trial
emulation). It is written automatically by the reconciliation engine from the
prescription — clinicians never edit it directly.

users.name: users

management_commands.name: invite_user
management_commands.help: Invite a user to the BGDDR registry by email.

users.models_module_doc:
User profile and invitation models for multi-user BGDDR deployment.

Extends Django's built-in User with registry-specific fields (role, department,
phone). Uses a one-to-one UserProfile so we don't replace the auth User model.

Invitation flow:
1. Admin creates an Invitation (email + role).
2. System emails a link with a token (or prints it for hand-delivery in clinics
   without reliable email).
3. User clicks link, sets password, account is created and role assigned.
